title: coreopsis acmella var. uliginosa
intro: Coreopsis acmella var. uliginosa, also known as marsh flea-blooms, is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and distinctive foliage. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat a variety of health issues. The health benefits of Coreopsis acmella var. uliginosa are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Coreopsis acmella var. uliginosa is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and salves, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
